metadata.title: Oh My OpenAgent
metadata.description: The Best Agent Harness. Meet Sisyphus: The Batteries-Included Agent that codes like you.

footer.copyright: © {year} Oh My OpenAgent. Open source under SUL-1.0.

ulw.title: ulw
ulw.headline: Type Three Letters. Walk Away.
ulw.description: Ultra Work mode triggers maximum precision: auto-planning, deep research, parallel agents, and self-correction loops. The system doesn't stop until it's done. You don't babysit.
ulw.terminalInput: ulw add authentication to the API
ulw.tagline: Zero intervention. Full autonomy. Just results.

sisyphus.title: Sisyphus
sisyphus.headline: The CTO Who Never Sleeps
sisyphus.description: Named after the mythological figure rolling his boulder uphill forever. Sisyphus parses implicit requirements, adapts to your codebase's maturity, and delegates to specialists. Interrupted? The boulder system resumes exactly where you left off.

intent.title: Intent Gate
intent.description: Parses what you meant, not just what you typed

explore.title: Codebase Assessment
explore.description: Maps your architecture before touching a line

delegate.title: Smart Delegation
delegate.description: Routes to the right specialist agent

sisyphus.boulderDescription: Active work tracked in boulder.json. Power outage? System crash? Doesn't matter. Pick up exactly where you stopped.

prometheusAtlas.title: Think, Then Act
prometheusAtlas.headline: Planning and Execution Are Different Jobs

prometheus.name: Prometheus
prometheus.description: The architect. Interviews you, explores your codebase, and creates detailed battle plans. Never writes code. Uses Metis and Momus as quality gates.

atlas.name: Atlas
atlas.description: The builder. Reads verified plans, delegates to specialized agents via category+skills system. Tracks learnings across tasks. Verifies independently — never trusts subagent claims.

metis.name: Metis
metis.description: Catches ambiguities before they become bugs.

momus.name: Momus
momus.description: Only says OKAY when the plan is actually perfect.

prometheusAtlas.whyItWorks: The planner doesn't code. The executor follows verified plans. Intelligence lives in the system, not individual agents.

hephaestus.title: Hephaestus
hephaestus.headline: Give Him a Goal, Not a Recipe
hephaestus.description: Named after the Greek god of the forge. Methodical, thorough, obsessive. Fires 2-5 parallel explore agents before writing any code. Built for deep architectural reasoning, complex debugging, and cross-domain synthesis.

teamMode.title: Team Mode
teamMode.headline: Multiple Agents. Side-by-Side. In Parallel.
teamMode.description: A lead agent orchestrates a team of category-specialized members, all running in parallel and communicating through dedicated tools. Watch every member work simultaneously in tmux. Opt-in — flip team_mode.enabled and restart.

lead.title: Lead Orchestration
lead.description: One coordinator. Multiple specialists. Real-time messaging via team_send_message and team_task_create.

parallel.title: True Parallelism
parallel.description: Up to 8 members work simultaneously. No waiting, no queues — every angle covered at once.

tmux.title: Tmux Visualization
tmux.description: Focus and grid windows let you zoom into one teammate without losing the bigger picture.

tools.title: team_* Tool Family
tools.description: 12 dedicated tools (team_create, team_status, team_send_message, ...) gated behind team_mode.enabled.

hyperplan.name: hyperplan
hyperplan.description: 5 hostile agents tear apart your plan from orthogonal angles before a single line of code is written.

securityResearch.name: security-research
securityResearch.description: 3 vulnerability hunters + 2 PoC engineers audit your codebase. Severity calibrated by actual exploitability.

teamMode.tagline: Off by default. Devastating when on.

agents.title: Specialized Agents
agents.subtitle: Five built-in agents for common workflows. Plus a dynamic system that assembles custom agents on demand.

oracle.name: Oracle
oracle.description: Complex debugging and architectural decisions. When the path forward isn't obvious.

librarian.name: Librarian
librarian.description: Finds real GitHub examples and official docs. Evidence-based, with permalinks.

explore.name: Explore
explore.description: Blazing fast codebase search. Cheap, parallel, always background.

metis.name: Metis
metis.description: Catches ambiguities in plans before they become production bugs.

momus.name: Momus
momus.description: Ruthless validation. Only approves when the plan is bulletproof.

dynamicSystem.name: Dynamic Agent
dynamicSystem.role: Assembled for the task at hand
dynamicSystem.description: Matches your request to the right model, loads the necessary skills, and builds an agent in real-time.

specialization.title: Specialization
specialization.description: Each agent does one thing exceptionally well. No jacks of all trades.

trustVerify.title: Trust But Verify
trustVerify.description: Orchestrator runs independent verification on everything. Subagents don't get a free pass.

wisdom.title: Wisdom Accumulation
wisdom.description: Learnings from each task passed to all subsequent tasks. The system gets smarter as it works.

modelOptimization.title: Model Optimization
modelOptimization.description: Expensive models for planning and complex decisions. Cheap models for routine work. Maximum output per dollar.

categories.title: Category System
categories.description: Intent-based routing. Say what you need — ultrabrain, visual-engineering, quick — and the right model handles it.

continuity.title: Session Continuity
continuity.description: Boulder system: interrupted work resumes exactly where it stopped. Zero context loss.

review1.text: It made me cancel my Cursor subscription. Unbelievable things are happening in the open source community.
review1.author: Arthur Guiot

review2.text: If Claude Code does in 7 days what a human does in 3 months, Sisyphus does it in 1 hour.
review2.author: B, Quant Researcher

review3.text: Knocked out 8000 eslint warnings with Oh My Opencode, just in a day.
review3.author: Jacob Ferrari

review4.text: use oh-my-openagent, you will never go back
review4.author: d0t3ch

review5.text: You guys should pull this into core and recruit him. Seriously. It's really, really, really good.
review5.author: Henning Kilset

review6.text: Oh My OpenCode Is Actually Insane
review6.author: Darren Builds AI (YouTube)

cta.title: Stop Writing Boilerplate
cta.subtitle: Install Oh My OpenAgent and let the agents handle it. Your first ulw command is 30 seconds away.

manifesto.autonomousCar: Imagine an autonomous car that requires you to grab the steering wheel every 30 seconds. Would you call that "autonomous"? No. You'd call it driver assist — barely better than cruise control.
manifesto.micromanagement: We've accepted a paradigm where "AI coding" means a chatbot that writes 20 lines, then waits for you to fix it. That's not automation — that's micromanagement.

painPoints.reviewing: Reviewing every single line for hallucinations

manifesto.notCollaboration: That's not "human-AI collaboration" — that's the AI failing to do its job.
manifesto.premise: {linkText} is built on the premise that the human should be the architect, not the spell-checker.

indistinguishable.title: Indistinguishable Code
indistinguishable.subtitle: Agent-written code should be indistinguishable from code written by a senior engineer.

items.patterns: Follows existing codebase patterns and architecture
items.errorHandling: Implements proper error handling and edge cases
items.tests: Writes tests that actually test behavior, not just coverage
items.noSlop: No 'AI slop' — clean, concise, maintainable code
items.comments: Comments only when they add value — never stating the obvious

indistinguishable.quote: "If you can tell whether a commit was made by a human or an agent, the agent has failed."

tokenCost.title: Token Cost vs. Productivity
tokenCost.description: We don't care about token usage. We care about output. If spending $5 on tokens saves an hour of engineering time, that's a 20x ROI.
tokenCost.optimizeDescription: We optimize for efficiency where it counts. Not by crippling the model, but by:
tokenCost.cheaperModels: Using cheaper models for routine tasks

cognitiveLoad.title: Minimize Human Cognitive Load
cognitiveLoad.subtitle: The human should only need to say what they want. Everything else is the agent's job.

ultrawork.title: Ultrawork
ultrawork.subtitle: Just say "ulw" and walk away.

steps.breakdown: Breaks down task into atomic steps

ultrawork.footer: Zero intervention. Full autonomy. Just results.

prometheus.title: Prometheus + Atlas
prometheus.subtitle: When you want strategic control.
prometheus.prometheusDescription: Conducts interview, researches context, and generates a detailed YAML plan.
prometheus.atlasDescription: Executes the plan, delegates to sub-agents, manages waves, and tracks progress.
prometheus.footer: You architect. Agents execute. Full transparency.

predictable.title: Predictable
predictable.description: Same inputs = consistent output. No random deviations or creative liberties unless requested.

continuous.title: Continuous
continuous.description: Survives interruptions. Tracks progress in real-time. Preserves context across sessions.

delegatable.title: Delegatable
delegatable.description: Clear acceptance criteria. Self-correcting mechanisms. Escalation only when absolutely needed.

metis.purpose: Catch ambiguities before they become bugs

momus.purpose: Verify plans are complete before execution

categorySystem.purpose: Route to optimal model without human decision

wisdomAccumulation.purpose: Learn from work, don't repeat mistakes

future.title: The Future We're Building

items.focus: Human developers focus on WHAT to build, not HOW to get AI to build it
items.quality: Code quality independent of who wrote it
items.complexity: Complex projects as easy as simple ones

future.quote1: "The agent should be invisible. Like electricity, like running water."
future.quote2: "You flip the switch. The light turns on. You don't think about the power grid."
